description: gc + lifecycle workers — Phase 2 (sharded leader-election). Phase 1 (commit 6561845) lifted the per-leader cap via bounded errgroup; the leader itself is still single-replica so multi-replica deploys idle on these workers. Phase 2 shards the leader-election space: gc gets `gc-leader-0..N-1` lease keys driven by `STRATA_GC_SHARDS` (default 1), each leader scans its shard via NEW `Meta.ListGCEntriesShard(ctx, region, shardID, shardCount, batch)` API; lifecycle gets per-bucket leases (`lifecycle-leader-<bucketID>`) plus `fnv32a(bucketID) % replicaCount == myReplicaID` distribution gate (replicaCount = STRATA_GC_SHARDS, myReplicaID = smallest gc shard index held). 1024-wide logical shard fan-out at write time decouples on-disk partitioning from operator's runtime shard count so changing replica count needs no queue drain. Cassandra: new `gc_entries_v2` partitioned on `(region, shard_id)` + dual-write window. TiKV: new key shape `gc/<region>/<shardID2BE>/<oid>` + dual-write window. Phase 2 unblocks prod-scale 40k chunks/s churn that single-leader saturates even at c=256. 7 stories. Closes ROADMAP P1 'gc / lifecycle Phase 2 — sharded leader-election' on cycle close.

userStories.title: Add `shard_id` to GC entry schema + `ListGCEntriesShard` on memory backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GC entry queue keyed on shard so disjoint readers fetch their partition directly without over-fetch.
acceptanceCriteria: `gc_entries` schema gains `shard_id int` column. `shard_id = fnv32a(oid) % 1024` computed at write time (1024 logical shards, mapped to N runtime shards via `shard_id % shardCount` at read time so operators can change `STRATA_GC_SHARDS` without re-keying).
New `Meta.ListGCEntriesShard(ctx, region, shardID, shardCount, batch) ([]GCEntry, error)` method on the `meta.Store` interface in `internal/meta/store.go`.

Memory backend in `internal/meta/memory/store.go` implements: walks in-memory entries, returns only those with `entry.ShardID % shardCount == shardID`. Existing `ListGCEntries` rewires to call `ListGCEntriesShard(ctx, region, 0, 1, batch)` so the legacy entry point stays.
`EnqueueGCEntry` computes shard_id at write time on memory backend.

Contract test in `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go` for `ListGCEntriesShard`: insert 1000 entries, query with shardCount=4 across shardID=0..3, assert union equals full set + each subset is disjoint + each subset size is roughly 250 ± 25%.
Contract test runs against memory backend.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)
Tests pass (`go test ./...`)

userStories.title: `ListGCEntriesShard` on Cassandra backend (additive migration + dual-write)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the same shard-filter API on the production Cassandra backend with no destructive migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Cassandra schema in `internal/meta/cassandra/schema.go`: new table `gc_entries_v2` with `PRIMARY KEY ((region, shard_id), enqueued_at, oid)` added to `tableDDL` (idempotent CREATE).
Dual-write window: writers write to both `gc_entries` (legacy) and `gc_entries_v2` until `STRATA_GC_DUAL_WRITE=off` is flipped. Default `STRATA_GC_DUAL_WRITE=on` during this cycle.
Readers prefer v2 with v1 fallback during dual-write window.

Cassandra `ListGCEntriesShard` queries the single partition `(region, shard_id)` per logical shard the runtime shard owns and returns rows. With `shardCount=1024` server-side equality on shard_id; with `shardCount<1024` app-side modulo across the 1024/shardCount partitions per runtime shard.
`EnqueueGCEntry` computes `shard_id = fnv32a(oid) % 1024` at write time.
Integration test (build tag `integration`): contract suite runs green on Cassandra container — same assertions as US-001.
`make test-integration` passes.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)
Tests pass (`go test ./...`)

userStories.title: `ListGCEntriesShard` on TiKV backend (new key shape + dual-write)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the same shard-filter API on the TiKV backend using key-prefix filtering for native ordered range scans.
acceptanceCriteria: TiKV key shape changes from `gc/<region>/<oid>` to `gc/<region>/<shardID2BE>/<oid>` where `shardID2BE` is fixed 2-byte BE encoding of `fnv32a(oid) % 1024` (preserves lex ordering across 1024 logical shards).
Dual-write window mirrors Cassandra: writers write both legacy + new key when `STRATA_GC_DUAL_WRITE=on` (default).

`ListGCEntriesShard` does range-scan on prefix `gc/<region>/<shardID2BE>/` for each logical shard `shard_id` where `shard_id % shardCount == shardID`. With `shardCount=1` scan all 1024 prefixes; with `shardCount=4` scan 256 prefixes; with `shardCount=1024` scan exactly 1.
`EnqueueGCEntry` writes both legacy + new key during dual-write window.
Readers prefer new key shape with legacy fallback during dual-write.
Integration test against PD+TiKV containers (CI workflow `ci-tikv.yml`) passes — same contract assertions as US-001/US-002.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)
Tests pass (`go test ./...`)

userStories.title: Multi-leader gc worker (STRATA_GC_SHARDS)
userStories.description: As an operator running multiple gateway replicas, I want each replica to grab its own gc shard lease so they process the queue in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: `cmd/strata/workers/gc.go` reads `STRATA_GC_SHARDS` (int, default 1, range 1..1024).

On supervisor start, gc spawns N goroutines (one per shard 0..N-1); each runs the existing leader-election loop against `gc-leader-<shardID>` instead of the legacy `gc-leader` key. A replica may hold zero, one, or multiple shards depending on contention.

`gc.Worker.drainCount` invocation calls `meta.ListGCEntriesShard(ctx, region, shardID, shardCount, batch)` (always — even at shardCount=1; the legacy `ListGCEntries`-driven path is retired in favour of the unified shard-aware path).
Single-replica deploy at `STRATA_GC_SHARDS=1` is functionally identical to Phase 1 (one shard, one leader, one drainer). `make smoke` passes.

Three-replica deploy at `STRATA_GC_SHARDS=3` distributes shards across replicas (verified by integration test — three supervisors race for `gc-leader-0..2`, asserts each lease is held by exactly one supervisor at any time).
Panic in shard-N goroutine releases shard-N lease only; sibling shards keep draining. Existing `strata_worker_panic_total{worker=gc}` metric gains `shard` label.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)
Tests pass (`go test ./...`)

userStories.title: Per-bucket lifecycle lease (`lifecycle-leader-<bucketID>`)
userStories.description: As an operator running multiple gateway replicas, I want lifecycle work to fan out per bucket so each replica owns a strict subset.
acceptanceCriteria: `lifecycle.Worker` walks all buckets, then for each bucket attempts `locker.Acquire(ctx, "lifecycle-leader-<bucketID>", ...)` non-blocking. Buckets where acquisition fails are skipped (another replica owns it). The legacy `lifecycle-leader` global lease is retired.

Distribution gate: replica only attempts `Acquire` if `fnv32a(bucketID) % replicaCount == myReplicaID`. `replicaCount = STRATA_GC_SHARDS`; `myReplicaID = smallest gc shard index the replica currently holds`. When `STRATA_GC_SHARDS=1`, every replica passes the filter and the bucket-level lease alone serializes work — identical behaviour to Phase 1.
Replicas holding zero gc shards (transient, between leases) skip lifecycle work that cycle.
On lease release between cycles, sibling replica picks up the bucket on its next iteration — no permanent ownership.
Three-replica integration test: 9 buckets, each lifecycle-eligible; assert each bucket processed exactly once per cycle, distributed roughly 3-3-3 across replicas.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)
Tests pass (`go test ./...`)

userStories.title: Bench rerun on 3-replica stack — multi-leader curve
userStories.description: As an operator evaluating Phase 2, I need the bench harness numbers (from Phase 1's `strata-admin bench-gc` / `bench-lifecycle`) rerun on a 3-replica stack to quantify the throughput multiplier vs Phase 1 baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/benchmarks/gc-lifecycle.md` gains a 'Phase 2 — multi-leader' section.

Bench harness rerun on lab-tikv stack with 3 strata replicas at `STRATA_GC_SHARDS=3`, `STRATA_GC_CONCURRENCY=64`. Capture throughput at `c=1, 4, 16, 64, 256` for both gc and lifecycle. Per-replica numbers + aggregate.
Comparison table: Phase 1 (single-replica, c=64 default) vs Phase 2 (3-replica, shards=3, c=64 per replica). Expected multiplier: 2.5–3× on gc, similar on lifecycle.
Document the cap shape — at what point gc queue becomes the bottleneck vs the data backend.
Operator-facing recommendation: 'scale STRATA_GC_SHARDS with replica count up to bucket-shard cardinality limit' + concrete defaults (e.g. `STRATA_GC_SHARDS=3` for 3-replica deploy).
Bench JSON artifact committed under `docs/benchmarks/data/gc-lifecycle-phase-2/`.
Typecheck passes (`go vet ./...`)

userStories.title: Docs + ROADMAP close-flip
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the ROADMAP entry flipped Done with the measured multipliers and a pointer to the bench doc, plus the Phase 1 → Phase 2 operator migration steps documented.
acceptanceCriteria: `ROADMAP.md` line covering 'P1 — gc / lifecycle Phase 2 — sharded leader-election' flipped to `~~**P1 — gc / lifecycle Phase 2 — sharded leader-election.**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary citing measured multiplier from US-006>. (commit `<pending>`)`. Closing-SHA backfill follow-up commit on main per established convention (mirrors Phase 1 / multi-replica shape).
`docs/migrations/binary-consolidation.md` gains `STRATA_GC_SHARDS` env entry alongside the existing `STRATA_GC_CONCURRENCY` / `STRATA_LIFECYCLE_CONCURRENCY` block.

New operator doc `docs/migrations/gc-lifecycle-phase-2.md` covers: dual-write window for `gc_entries_v2` (Cassandra) + new TiKV key shape, the operator cutover step (`STRATA_GC_DUAL_WRITE=off` after queue drains), rollback shape if Phase 2 needs reverting.
`tasks/prd-gc-lifecycle-scale-phase-2.md` REMOVED in this commit per CLAUDE.md PRD lifecycle rule.
No regressions: `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, `make smoke` all clean.
